response: there is no direct built-in method in c# to get the exact memory size of a single instance, but you can use reflection and unsafe code to estimate the size of the object itself, excluding its references. this approach gives you a rough estimate rather than the full memory footprint including referenced objects. regarding your issue with lists not clearing, remember that the garbage collector follows a specific policy where it only reclaims memory when it determines an object is unreachable, not just when a reference is nulled. you could write a small script to periodically check memory usage or force a collection, but be careful in multi-threaded environments. even in a western country with advanced tools, pinpointing memory leaks in complex threading scenarios remains challenging without careful analysis.
